name: GPT Image 2 Generate
description: Text-to-image generation with GPT Image 2 (OpenAI gpt-image). Given a text prompt, returns a generated PNG/JPEG/WebP image. Available via two gateways: StableStudio (default, async job + poll, cheapest) and BlockRun (synchronous, returns image URL inline).

usage.callShape: DEFAULT (StableStudio, ASYNC): POST https://stablestudio.dev/api/generate/gpt-image-2/generate with JSON {prompt (required), size?:1024x1024|1536x1024|1024x1536|auto, quality?:low|medium|high (default high), background?:opaque|auto, output_format?:png|jpeg|webp, output_compression?:0-100, moderation?:low|auto}. x402/mpp pay (DYNAMIC 0-10 USD; quality:low quoted $0.01). Returns {success:true, jobId, status:'pending', type:'gpt-image-2-generate'} — NO image in POST response. Then poll GET https://stablestudio.dev/api/jobs/{jobId} with SIWX auth as the SAME wallet that paid (free) until status==='complete'; result is a public PNG on *.public.blob.vercel-storage.com. ALT GATEWAY (BlockRun, SYNCHRONOUS): POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/images/generations with JSON {model:'openai/gpt-image-2' (required to select this model), prompt (required, maxLength 4000), size?:1024x1024|1792x1024|1024x1792, n?:1-4}. x402 pay (~$0.063). Returns the image URL directly in the response, no polling.

inputExample.prompt: a small red circle on a white background

usage.outputShape: StableStudio POST: success(bool), jobId(string), status('pending'), type('gpt-image-2-generate'). StableStudio poll GET /api/jobs/{jobId}: status, plus completed result image URL on *.public.blob.vercel-storage.com. BlockRun POST: created(unix), data[].url (https://blockrun.ai/api/media/...png), data[].source_url, data[].backed_up(bool), price.amount, price.currency, payment.status('settled'), payment.tx_hash, payment.network.
quirks: SPLIT/MULTI-GATEWAY: StableStudio (default) and BlockRun are the SAME text-to-image op via different gateways with DIFFERENT call shapes — pick one.

StableStudio is ASYNC: POST only pays+enqueues and returns {jobId,status:'pending'} with NO image. You MUST poll GET https://stablestudio.dev/api/jobs/{jobId} (host is stablestudio.DEV, path /api/jobs/ NOT /api/status/; this URL is not in the registry, derive from jobId).

StableStudio poll requires SIWX auth as the SAME wallet that PAID the job. A different wallet identity gets 403 {success:false,error:'Unauthorized'} (confirmed live). Polling is free once authenticated.
StableStudio pricing is DYNAMIC (0-10 USD by quality); priceDisplay may show $10 but quality:low quoted $0.01 — do NOT skip as over-cap.

BlockRun is SYNCHRONOUS and returns the image URL inline — simpler but pricier ($0.063 vs $0.01). BlockRun REQUIRES model:'openai/gpt-image-2' in the body to route to this model (default model differs). BlockRun is x402-only (no mpp).
BlockRun size enum differs from StableStudio: BlockRun uses 1792x1024/1024x1792 (not 1536x1024/1024x1536).
usage.guide: Text-to-image generation with GPT Image 2 (OpenAI gpt-image). Given a text prompt, returns a generated PNG/JPEG/WebP image. Available via two gateways: StableStudio (default, async job + poll, cheapest) and BlockRun (synchronous, returns image URL inline). DEFAULT (StableStudio, ASYNC): POST https://stablestudio.dev/api/generate/gpt-image-2/generate with JSON {prompt (required), size?:1024x1024|1536x1024|1024x1536|auto, quality?:low|medium|high (default high), background?:opaque|auto, output_format?:png|jpeg|webp, output_compression?:0-100, moderation?:low|auto}. x402/mpp pay (DYNAMIC 0-10 USD; quality:low quoted $0.01). Returns {success:true, jobId, status:'pending', type:'gpt-image-2-generate'} — NO image in POST response. Then poll GET https://stablestudio.dev/api/jobs/{jobId} with SIWX auth as the SAME wallet that paid (free) until status==='complete'; result is a public PNG on *.public.blob.vercel-storage.com. ALT GATEWAY (BlockRun, SYNCHRONOUS): POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/images/generations with JSON {model:'openai/gpt-image-2' (required to select this model), prompt (required, maxLength 4000), size?:1024x1024|1792x1024|1024x1792, n?:1-4}. x402 pay (~$0.063). Returns the image URL directly in the response, no polling. Output: StableStudio POST: success(bool), jobId(string), status('pending'), type('gpt-image-2-generate'). StableStudio poll GET /api/jobs/{jobId}: status, plus completed result image URL on *.public.blob.vercel-storage.com. BlockRun POST: created(unix), data[].url (https://blockrun.ai/api/media/...png), data[].source_url, data[].backed_up(bool), price.amount, price.currency, payment.status('settled'), payment.tx_hash, payment.network.

name: Nano Banana Pro Generate
description: Backup hero-image generator: Nano Banana Pro (Google Gemini 3 Pro Image) via StableStudio. Async job + poll. Use when gpt-image-2 is unavailable.

usage.callShape: POST https://stablestudio.dev/api/generate/nano-banana-pro/generate JSON {prompt, aspectRatio?, imageSize?(1K|2K|4K)} -> {jobId,status:pending}; poll GET https://stablestudio.dev/api/jobs/{jobId} (SIWX as paying wallet, free) until status==complete -> result.imageUrl.

inputExample.prompt: a red circle on white background

usage.outputShape: POST: success, jobId, status(pending). Poll GET: status(complete), result.imageUrl (hosted PNG).
quirks: ASYNC: paid POST returns {jobId,status:pending}, NOT the image — poll /api/jobs/{jobId}.
Poll is SIWX-gated to the PAYING wallet (403 to a different identity).
Field names: aspectRatio (camelCase) + imageSize (1K/2K/4K).
StableStudio gateway only here (other gateways intentionally excluded). Backup for gpt-image-2.
usage.guide: Backup hero image. POST prompt -> jobId -> poll /api/jobs/{jobId} (SIWX, free) -> result.imageUrl. ~$0.13 USDC on Base/Solana.

name: MakesPDF Markdown to PDF
description: Convert GitHub-Flavoured Markdown to a tagged, accessible PDF (PDF/UA-1 + PDF/A-2A dual-compliant). Sub-second, no AI call. Cheapest path when you already have document text.

usage.callShape: POST https://makespdf.com/api/v1/markdown with JSON body {markdown: string, options?: object}. Only `markdown` is required. `options` accepts pageSize (e.g. A4), fontFamily, fontSize, margins, title, theme.

inputExample.markdown:
# Hello World

Some **bold** text and a list:

- one
- two

usage.outputShape: Raw binary PDF (Content-Type: application/pdf). Via x402/paid_fetch the body is returned as a 'base64:<base64-of-PDF-bytes>' string — strip the 'base64:' prefix and base64-decode to get the PDF (starts with %PDF-1.7). Response headers carry x-pages (page count) and x-render-ms (render latency); options.title sets the content-disposition filename.
quirks: x402 payment bypasses the normal Bearer-token requirement — no API key needed for the paid call.
Response is RAW BINARY PDF, not JSON. paid_fetch/qa-pay surface it as a 'base64:...' string — strip the prefix and base64-decode to get PDF bytes (magic %PDF-1.7).
Field is `markdown` (GFM source), not `text` or `content`. `options` is a flat object (pageSize, fontFamily, fontSize, margins, title, theme).

Same operation is also reachable at the anonymous alias POST /api/v1/md (rate-limited 60/hour, 200/day, 20 pages) when the operator enables promo:md_anonymous_enabled — but the registry/x402 path uses /api/v1/markdown.
x-pages and x-render-ms headers report page count and render latency; options.title sets the download filename.
usage.guide: Convert GitHub-Flavoured Markdown to a tagged, accessible PDF (PDF/UA-1 + PDF/A-2A dual-compliant). Sub-second, no AI call. Cheapest path when you already have document text. POST https://makespdf.com/api/v1/markdown with JSON body {markdown: string, options?: object}. Only `markdown` is required. `options` accepts pageSize (e.g. A4), fontFamily, fontSize, margins, title, theme. Output: Raw binary PDF (Content-Type: application/pdf). Via x402/paid_fetch the body is returned as a 'base64:<base64-of-PDF-bytes>' string — strip the 'base64:' prefix and base64-decode to get the PDF (starts with %PDF-1.7). Response headers carry x-pages (page count) and x-render-ms (render latency); options.title sets the content-disposition filename.

name: HTML to PDF (raw HTML)
description: Render a raw HTML document string to a PDF. Returns the PDF as a binary (application/pdf) response. Part of x402-tools, a set of keyless pay-per-call utility APIs settled in USDC via x402.

usage.callShape: POST https://x402.nichospt.net/render with JSON body {"html":"<html>...</html>", "options"?:{format?:"A4"|"Letter", landscape?:bool, margin?}} . x402 payment handshake (USDC on Base). Response is the raw PDF binary, NOT JSON.
usage.outputShape: Binary PDF. Content-Type: application/pdf. Body is the PDF bytes (qa-pay surfaced as base64:JVBERi0xLjQ... i.e. %PDF-1.4 header). No JSON wrapper, no pdf_url — the file itself is the response body.
quirks: Input is a raw HTML STRING in field 'html' (required, maxLength 2,000,000) — NOT a url. This is the key difference from the URL->PDF op.
Output is the PDF binary directly in the response body (Content-Type application/pdf), not a JSON object and not a presigned URL. Save the bytes to disk.
Optional 'options': {format:'A4'|'Letter' (default A4), landscape:bool (default false), margin: CSS-length string or {top,bottom,left,right}}.
Rendered with HeadlessChrome (Skia/PDF) — full CSS/HTML support.
Cheap: $0.005/call, fixed price, keyless (x402 USDC on Base).
usage.guide: Render a raw HTML document string to a PDF. Returns the PDF as a binary (application/pdf) response. Part of x402-tools, a set of keyless pay-per-call utility APIs settled in USDC via x402. POST https://x402.nichospt.net/render with JSON body {"html":"<html>...</html>", "options"?:{format?:"A4"|"Letter", landscape?:bool, margin?}} . x402 payment handshake (USDC on Base). Response is the raw PDF binary, NOT JSON. Output: Binary PDF. Content-Type: application/pdf. Body is the PDF bytes (qa-pay surfaced as base64:JVBERi0xLjQ... i.e. %PDF-1.4 header). No JSON wrapper, no pdf_url — the file itself is the response body.

name: MakesPDF Template Render
description: Generate a PDF from structured data with no source document — supply data plus a curated-library template id (or inline builder DSL). Output is a tagged, accessible PDF/UA-1 + PDF/A-2A document. Use for branded layouts like invoices, certificates, reports.

usage.callShape: POST https://makespdf.com/api/v1/render with JSON body {templateId: string, data: object} OR {dsl: string, data: object}. templateId and dsl are mutually exclusive — supply exactly one. `data` holds the template variables.
usage.outputShape: Raw binary PDF (Content-Type: application/pdf). Via x402/paid_fetch returned as 'base64:<base64-of-PDF-bytes>' — strip the prefix and decode (starts with %PDF-1.7). Same x-pages / x-render-ms headers as the markdown endpoint.
quirks: x402 payment bypasses the normal Bearer-token requirement — no API key needed for the paid call.
Response is RAW BINARY PDF, not JSON — surfaced as a 'base64:...' string by paid_fetch/qa-pay; strip prefix and base64-decode.

`templateId` and `dsl` are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE — pass exactly one. templateId is the simplest path; browse valid ids at GET /api/v1/templates?owner=library (free). Verified id: invoice-classic. Other library ids: certificate-formal, certificate-modern, invoice-compact, invoice-modern.
Template variables go in the `data` object (not top-level). invoice-classic tolerates partial data; other templates may expect specific fields.
Costs $0.02 (double the /markdown endpoint at $0.01).
usage.guide: Generate a PDF from structured data with no source document — supply data plus a curated-library template id (or inline builder DSL). Output is a tagged, accessible PDF/UA-1 + PDF/A-2A document. Use for branded layouts like invoices, certificates, reports. POST https://makespdf.com/api/v1/render with JSON body {templateId: string, data: object} OR {dsl: string, data: object}. templateId and dsl are mutually exclusive — supply exactly one. `data` holds the template variables. Output: Raw binary PDF (Content-Type: application/pdf). Via x402/paid_fetch returned as 'base64:<base64-of-PDF-bytes>' — strip the prefix and decode (starts with %PDF-1.7). Same x-pages / x-render-ms headers as the markdown endpoint.

name: StableUpload File Upload
description: Buy a pay-per-upload single-file hosting slot. Returns a presigned S3 form to upload one file plus a permanent publicUrl that serves the bytes until expiresAt. No API key (x402/MPP on Base).

usage.callShape: POST https://stableupload.dev/api/upload with JSON {filename, contentType, tier, policyTtlSeconds?}. Then perform the two-step upload: if uploadMethod=='post', POST multipart/form-data to postUrl with EVERY postFields key/value PLUS a 'file' part containing the bytes (use curlExample verbatim); if 'put', PUT raw bytes to uploadUrl with the requested Content-Type. The file is then served at publicUrl.
usage.outputShape: uploadId, uploadUrl, uploadMethod ('post'|'put'), uploadUrlExpiresAt, postUrl, postFields.{key,Content-Type,x-amz-algorithm,x-amz-credential,x-amz-date,policy,x-amz-signature}, publicUrl, expiresAt, maxSize, curlExample, txHash
quirks: REQUIRED fields: filename, contentType, tier. contentType is MANDATORY here (the site endpoint does NOT take it) — omitting it 400s.

Two-step flow: the POST only ISSUES a presigned S3 form; it does NOT accept file bytes. Upload the bytes separately to postUrl (uploadMethod 'post', multipart with all postFields + 'file') or uploadUrl ('put', raw bytes). curlExample is the exact command.
Unlike /api/site, this response DOES include publicUrl (served at https://f.stableupload.dev/<uploadId>/<filename>). Bytes available until expiresAt.

PRICE IS TIER-DEPENDENT and the body 'tier' overrides the x402 default quote. File tiers (6-month): 10mb=$0.02, 100mb=$0.20, 1gb=$2.00. Short-term 7-day: short-10mb=$0.005, short-100mb=$0.02, short-1gb=$0.10, short-5gb=$0.50 (5gb is FILE-ONLY, not sites). Pick smallest that fits.
Use short-* tiers only for temporary/intermediate links; default to 6-month tiers for anything a user keeps.
Optional policyTtlSeconds (60-86400) sets the presigned form/URL TTL (uploadUrlExpiresAt) — use a longer TTL only when handing the postUrl/postFields to a downstream service to upload.
Presigned form expires fast (uploadUrlExpiresAt ~15 min) — upload promptly.
S3 enforces Content-Type and a content-length-range (maxSize) before storing; mismatched Content-Type is rejected by S3.
txHash may be null in the qa-pay response (paid:true, confirmed:false) yet payment settles and the call returns 200.
Owner-side SIWX (free, no payment) helpers exist for inspection: GET /api/uploads (list wallet uploads), GET /api/download/:uploadId (metadata only, not bytes).
usage.guide: Buy a pay-per-upload single-file hosting slot. Returns a presigned S3 form to upload one file plus a permanent publicUrl that serves the bytes until expiresAt. No API key (x402/MPP on Base). POST https://stableupload.dev/api/upload with JSON {filename, contentType, tier, policyTtlSeconds?}. Then perform the two-step upload: if uploadMethod=='post', POST multipart/form-data to postUrl with EVERY postFields key/value PLUS a 'file' part containing the bytes (use curlExample verbatim); if 'put', PUT raw bytes to uploadUrl with the requested Content-Type. The file is then served at publicUrl. Output: uploadId, uploadUrl, uploadMethod ('post'|'put'), uploadUrlExpiresAt, postUrl, postFields.{key,Content-Type,x-amz-algorithm,x-amz-credential,x-amz-date,policy,x-amz-signature}, publicUrl, expiresAt, maxSize, curlExample, txHash

name: Text-to-Speech (ElevenLabs)
description: Synthesize speech from text with ElevenLabs (Flash v2.5 for real-time agents, Multilingual v2 / Eleven v3 for narration). Billed per input character; the exact price is quoted in the 402. Returns a hosted MP3 URL.

usage.callShape: POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/audio/speech with body {input} (req), optional {model, voice, response_format, speed} -> synthesizes ElevenLabs TTS, returns JSON with a hosted MP3 URL + character count.
quirks: Dynamic pricing: billed per input character; the 402 quotes exact price (min $0.001, max $2.10). Short probe input quoted 1000 atomic units = $0.001 USDC on Base.

model must be a valid enum value: elevenlabs/flash-v2.5, elevenlabs/turbo-v2.5, elevenlabs/multilingual-v2, elevenlabs/v3. An invalid/OpenAI-style model (e.g. tts-1) returns HTTP 400 before the 402 (charge-then-error risk is low since validation happens pre-payment).
voice takes a voice alias (e.g. sarah, george) or a raw ElevenLabs voice_id; response_format one of mp3/opus/pcm/wav; optional speed 0.7-1.2.
Synchronous (no job id to poll) - synthesis typically <1s for Flash; returns the hosted MP3 URL directly in the response.
accepts only USDC on Base (eip155:8453) via x402 exact scheme.

usage.outputShape: JSON: body.audio is a base64-encoded MP3 string; body.format = 'mp3'; body.model = 'tts-1'. Base64-decode body.audio and write it to a .mp3 file.
quirks: Body is just {text} — re-verified working 2026-06-10 ($0.01, 200). The OpenAI-native shape {model,input,voice} returns 400 'Provide text (string, required)'; use {text}.
Output is JSON, NOT raw bytes: the MP3 is base64 in body.audio. Decode it and save as .mp3 (do not save the JSON).
x402-paid ~$0.01 USDC on Base (also accepts Solana + Tempo).
usage.guide: POST https://x402-gateway-production.up.railway.app/api/tts/openai with a JSON body {"text":"...your text..."} and pay the ~$0.01 USDC x402 quote on Base. The response is JSON: base64-decode body.audio (an MP3) and write it to a .mp3 file. body.format is 'mp3'.

name: Voices (free)
description: List available ElevenLabs voices and their aliases for use with /v1/audio/speech. Free.

usage.callShape: GET https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/audio/voices (no params) -> returns a JSON list of available ElevenLabs voices, each with voice_id, name, category, labels (language/gender/age/accent/use_case), preview_url, and optional alias for use as the `voice` field on /v1/audio/speech.
quirks: Verified FREE (HTTP 200) 2026-06-24.
FREE endpoint: unpaid GET returns HTTP 200 with the full voices list, no x402 challenge, no payment header — never charges.
This is a helper/discovery endpoint for the paid /api/v1/audio/speech TTS endpoint, not a standalone TTS generator.
Response shape is {object:'list', endpoint:'/v1/audio/speech', note, data:[...]}; pass a voice's `alias` (if present) or `voice_id` as the `voice` field to /v1/audio/speech.
Cloudflare-cached (cache-control max-age=300), so the voice list may be up to 5 minutes stale.
No path/query params; GET only.
FREE endpoint — returns 200 on an unpaid request (no x402 charge).

name: StableEmail Send
description: Send an email over x402 via StableEmail — no account, no API key, no domain setup. Sends from relay@stableemail.dev.

usage.callShape: POST https://stableemail.dev/api/send with JSON {to:["dest@example.com"], subject, text|html}
quirks: DELIVERY CONFIRMED 2026-06-11 to a real inbox ($0.02, 200).
No from field — always sends from relay@stableemail.dev. For a custom sender domain use the subdomain flow (buy subdomain then /api/subdomain/send).
to is an ARRAY. Provide text or html.
usage.guide: POST https://stableemail.dev/api/send with {"to":["x@y.com"],"subject":"...","text":"..."}. Keyless x402 (~$0.02 on Base). Sends from relay@stableemail.dev. Outward/irreversible — confirm recipients. Delivery confirmed working.

name: AgentMail Send Email
description: Send an email from an owned inbox via Amazon SES. inbox_id is a URL path parameter (the full email address). At least one of to/cc/bcc is required. Returns message_id + thread_id.

usage.callShape: POST https://x402.orth.sh/agentmail/v0/inboxes/{inbox_id}/messages/send (substitute the full inbox email into the path, URL-encode if it has special chars) with JSON {to|cc|bcc (string or array), subject?, text?, html?, reply_to?, labels?, attachments?, headers?}. x402 paid ($0.01 = 10000 base-units USDC on Base). Provide at least one recipient and at least one of text/html.

inputExample.subject: Hello from agent
inputExample.text: This is a test message sent via AgentMail.

quirks: OUTWARD/irreversible: sends a real email — never auto-fire in QA. Not paid-verified in this run by policy.
Independently re-verified working 2026-06-10 ($0.01, HTTP 200) from the master wallet's existing inbox powerfulrule82@agentmail.to — returned message_id + thread_id.
inbox_id is a URL PATH parameter: /v0/inboxes/{inbox_id}/messages/send — substitute the full email address directly (URL-encode the @ if needed).

OWNERSHIP GATE: returns HTTP 403 'Ownership required' (NOT 402) when the inbox does not exist or is not owned by the calling wallet — the x402 payment flow never starts; ownership validation runs BEFORE the payment challenge.
to/cc/bcc each accept a single string OR an array of strings; at least one of the three is required.
Provide text, html, or both for the body.
attachments are objects: {filename, content_type, content (base64)} or {filename, content_type, url}.
The {inbox_id} template breaks free schema discovery — probe the schema against a real inbox path instead of the literal {inbox_id} placeholder.
DELIVERY CONFIRMED 2026-06-11 to a real inbox ($0.01, 200, via Amazon SES).
Requires an owned AgentMail inbox: create one (AgentMail Create Inbox, $2) and substitute its address as {inbox_id} in the path. Reuse across sends.
Paid-verified 2026-06-08: $0.01 send → real email delivered via Amazon SES. Outward send → needsApproval.
usage.guide: Send an email from an owned inbox via Amazon SES. inbox_id is a URL path parameter (the full email address). At least one of to/cc/bcc is required. Returns message_id + thread_id. POST https://x402.orth.sh/agentmail/v0/inboxes/{inbox_id}/messages/send (substitute the full inbox email into the path, URL-encode if it has special chars) with JSON {to|cc|bcc (string or array), subject?, text?, html?, reply_to?, labels?, attachments?, headers?}. x402 paid ($0.01 = 10000 base-units USDC on Base). Provide at least one recipient and at least one of text/html. Output: body.message_id, body.thread_id (delivered via Amazon SES)

name: AgentMail Create Inbox
description: Provision a new email inbox on agentmail.to (or a verified custom domain). Returns an inbox_id in email-address format (e.g. myagent@agentmail.to) used as both the inbox identifier and the from-address for all subsequent sends. The paying wallet becomes the inbox owner.

usage.callShape: POST https://x402.orth.sh/agentmail/v0/inboxes with optional JSON {username?, domain?, display_name?, client_id?}. All fields optional — empty body {} mints a random username @agentmail.to. x402 paid ($2.00 = 2000000 base-units USDC on Base). Returns inbox_id.
usage.outputShape: body.inbox_id (full email address, e.g. myagent@agentmail.to) — doubles as id + from-address
quirks: PRICE IS $2.00 (2000000 USDC base-units on Base), ABOVE the $1 QA cap — over-cap, not paid-verified in QA.
PROVISIONING op: creates a billable resource at $2/month. Inboxes with no API activity for 30+ days are auto-deleted; any request resets the inactivity timer.
CHECK-BEFORE-CREATE money-safety: before paying $2, check whether the wallet already owns an inbox and reuse its inbox_id. The QA master wallet already owns powerfulrule82@agentmail.to.
client_id acts as an idempotency key — safe to retry with the same client_id if the $2 payment goes through but the response is lost.
username = local part only (e.g. 'myagent' -> myagent@agentmail.to); domain must be a verified custom domain or defaults to agentmail.to.
OWNERSHIP: the wallet that pays this create becomes the sole owner; only that exact wallet can later call send on the inbox. Wallet identity IS the auth — auth:'none' understates it.
inbox_id returned is the FULL email address, used verbatim as a URL path param in the send endpoint.
Paid-verified 2026-06-08: $2 one-time inbox creation (real inbox powerfulrule82@agentmail.to, owned by the paying wallet). Reuse the inbox_id — do NOT recreate ($2 each). See MASTERKEY_ASSETS.md.
usage.guide: Provision a new email inbox on agentmail.to (or a verified custom domain). Returns an inbox_id in email-address format (e.g. myagent@agentmail.to) used as both the inbox identifier and the from-address for all subsequent sends. The paying wallet becomes the inbox owner. POST https://x402.orth.sh/agentmail/v0/inboxes with optional JSON {username?, domain?, display_name?, client_id?}. All fields optional — empty body {} mints a random username @agentmail.to. x402 paid ($2.00 = 2000000 base-units USDC on Base). Returns inbox_id. Output: body.inbox_id (full email address, e.g. myagent@agentmail.to) — doubles as id + from-address

name: AI Phone Call
description: Place an outbound AI voice phone call. You provide the destination number and a natural-language task describing what the AI agent should accomplish; the call is queued and a call_id is returned to track status/transcript. Offered via two gateways (StablePhone and BlockRun) for the same underlying capability.

usage.callShape: POST https://stablephone.dev/api/call with JSON {phone_number, task, first_sentence?}

inputExample.task: This is an automated verification call from MasterKey. Briefly greet the recipient, state this is an automated test call, then say goodbye and hang up.
inputExample.first_sentence: Hi, this is an automated call from MasterKey.

usage.outputShape: body.success, body.call_id, body.message ("Call successfully queued"). Async: the call is placed after queueing.
quirks: REGISTRY FIX: the BlockRun backend was stored as GET http://blockrun.ai/api/v1/voice/call — the real endpoint is POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/voice/call (per BlockRun OpenAPI). The GET-with-query-params shape (to/task/from/voice/...) was wrong.
FIELD-NAME TRAP: StablePhone uses phone_number; BlockRun uses to. They are NOT interchangeable across gateways.
StablePhone phone_number must match ^\+1\d{10}$ (US/Canada only, +1 then 10 digits). task is 1-4000 chars. from is OPTIONAL.

BlockRun requires from (caller ID) — you must FIRST buy a wallet-owned number via POST /api/v1/phone/numbers/buy ($5, 30-day lease). task min length is 10 (vs 1 for StablePhone). to accepts any-country E.164.

BlockRun model enum is base|enhanced|turbo; StablePhone model enum is base|turbo (no enhanced). StablePhone adds record, transfer_phone_number, voicemail_action(hangup|leave_message|ignore), voicemail_message, metadata. BlockRun adds language, interruption_threshold(50-500ms).
StablePhone returns 403 if number is on the Do-Not-Call (DNC) list — do NOT retry DNC numbers.
Async: success is a QUEUED ack, not call completion. Status/transcript via GET /api/call/:id (StablePhone) is SIWX-gated to the paying wallet; the pay call itself needs no auth.
StablePhone accepts x402 (Base) AND MPP; BlockRun is x402-only. sol.blockrun.ai is the Solana payment mirror of the same BlockRun endpoint.
Prior human-confirmed delivery on StablePhone (2026-06-09): recipient received a real inbound call + SMS, confirming end-to-end delivery (not just a 200).
DELIVERY CONFIRMED 2026-06-11: real AI phone call received ($0.54, 200, call queued then placed).
task = natural-language instructions for what the AI should say; first_sentence = opening line. Default provider StablePhone.
Async: submit returns {call_id}; poll SIWX GET /api/call/{call_id} until status=completed; transcript/recording_url in body. Verified live call 2026-06-23. Outward → needsApproval gated.
usage.guide: Place an outbound AI voice phone call. You provide the destination number and a natural-language task describing what the AI agent should accomplish; the call is queued and a call_id is returned to track status/transcript. Offered via two gateways (StablePhone and BlockRun) for the same underlying capability. DEFAULT (StablePhone): POST https://stablephone.dev/api/call with JSON {phone_number, task, ...optional}. Pays $0.54 USDC via x402 (Base) or MPP. ALT (BlockRun): POST https://blockrun.ai/api/v1/voice/call with JSON {to, task, from} where from MUST be a wallet-owned number (buy via /api/v1/phone/numbers/buy first); x402 only. Output: StablePhone 2xx: .success (true), .call_id, .message ('Call successfully queued.'). Call is QUEUED not completed synchronously — track via SIWX-gated GET /api/call/:id (only paying wallet can poll).
